informational.description: User wants to understand a concept or process

navigational.description: User is looking for a specific resource or tool

transactional.description: User is ready to take an action or make a purchase

commercial_investigation.description: User is researching before making a decision

long_tail_query_patterns.description: Templates for constructing long-tail keyword targets that signal specificity and avoid head-term competition

example_transformations.better: solar panel ROI calculation for homeowners with time-of-use electricity rates
example_transformations.rationale: The improved query targets users with a specific financial decision to make, not generic curiosity. Conversion intent is higher, competition is lower.

experience: Include a first-person observation or result where applicable
Reference a specific test, measurement, or hands-on outcome
Name specific constraints that were encountered
expertise: Use the correct technical terminology for the niche — defined in context
Address edge cases and failure modes
Distinguish between beginner and advanced interpretations of the same concept
authoritativeness: Reference primary sources (studies, standards, official documentation) where they exist
Name specific tool versions, industry bodies, or certification standards
Include quantified outcomes, not qualitative claims alone
trustworthiness: Acknowledge limitations and conditions under which the advice does not apply
Do not make absolute claims without qualification
Cite uncertainty where it genuinely exists — this signals intellectual honesty

semantic_triplet_guidelines.description: Subject-Predicate-Object structures that Google's NLP can extract as named entities and relationships
good_examples: Solar inverters convert DC electricity into grid-compatible AC power.
Python's GIL prevents true multithreading in CPU-bound tasks.
BERT tokenizers split words into subword units using WordPiece encoding.
bad_examples: This is really important to understand.
There are many ways to approach this.
It can be challenging to deal with.
rules: Name the subject explicitly — avoid 'this', 'it', 'they' as subjects

deep.use_case: Pillar content, cluster hub, or competitive SERP target
